data.title: UPS Earnings: United Parcel Stock Jumps on Q4 EPS & Revenue Beats
data.description:
United Parcel (UPS) stock rallied on Tuesday following the release of the delivery company's Q4 2025 earnings report. The company reported adjusted earnings per share of $2.38, which beat Wall Street's estimate of $2.20. However, the company's adjusted EPS fell 13.4% year-over-year from $2.75.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Revenue reported by United Parcel came in at $24.5 billion, which was above analysts' estimate of $24.01 billion for the quarter. Despite this beat, the company's revenue was down 2.9% compared to the $25.24 billion reported in the fourth quarter of 2024. The company's U.S. Domestic Segment revenue dropped 3.2% year-over-year to $16.76 billion from $17.31 billion, though revenue per piece increased grew by 8.3%.
data.author: William White

data.title: 3 Best Value Stocks with Over 20% Upside in 2026, According to Analysts
data.description:
Investors looking for undervalued stocks in the current volatile market may want to pay close attention to Wall Street's latest picks. These stocks stand out for their solid fundamentals, attractive valuations, and catalysts that could drive meaningful gains in the months ahead.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Before we go further, let's define value stocks. These are companies that look undervalued compared to their true worth, trading at lower price-to-earnings or price-to-book ratios. When the market eventually recognizes their real value, they can deliver strong gains.

Now, let's check the three value stocks that analysts have spotlighted. Each carries a "Strong Buy" consensus rating but also offers more than 20% upside potential from current levels.

Here Are This Week's Stocks

LKQ (LKQ) – LKQ is a global provider of alternative and specialty auto parts, supplying recycled, refurbished, and aftermarket components to the automotive repair industry. It has a Strong Buy analyst consensus rating and an average price target of $41.25, implying a 22.15% upside potential from the current levels. The company's P/E of 12.63x reflects a 37.9% discount to the Consumer Cyclical sector's median of 20.34.

The company's board of directors has launched a strategic review that includes exploring a potential sale of the entire company. The process follows pressure from activist investor Ananym Capital, which has urged LKQ to sell parts of its business, including its European operations, to boost shareholder returns.

United Airlines (UAL) – United Airlines is a major U.S. airline that operates domestic and international passenger and cargo flights worldwide. Its average price target of $141.79 implies a 31.58% upside potential from the current levels. UAL stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating. Trading at a P/E of 10.53x, the company is valued 60.6% below the Industrials sector's median multiple of 26.72.

Last week, the company reported mixed results for the fourth quarter of 2025. The performance was driven by higher travel demand, boosting sales of both premium seats and basic tickets.

Alkermes (ALKS) – This biopharmaceutical company develops and commercializes medicines for neuroscience and oncology. It has a Strong Buy analyst consensus rating and an average price target of $46.17, implying a 39.44% upside potential from the current levels. With a P/E ratio of 16.38x, the stock is priced at a 42.1% discount to the Healthcare sector's median of 28.31.

The company recently highlighted strong momentum for ALKS 2680, its lead orexin‑2 receptor agonist, which is moving into Phase 3 trials for narcolepsy after positive Phase 2 data and an FDA Breakthrough Therapy designation.

What Is TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter?

TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter helps investors identify high-potential value stocks with strong fundamentals and long-term growth potential, based on TipRanks' data and analysis. The newsletter, published weekly, includes macroeconomic, market-wide, and company-specific analysis to help investors understand the trends that affect value investing.

Stay ahead of the market – subscribe now!
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: UNH Earnings: UnitedHealth Stock Drops as Medicare Pricing Overshadows Revenue Growth
data.description:
Shares of UnitedHealth Group (UNH) fell more than 12% in premarket trading after the company reported its 2025 results. The company posted consolidated revenue of $447.6 billion for 2025, up 12% year over year. However, the stock came under pressure after the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) proposed a Medicare Advantage payment increase of just 0.09% for 2027, far below market expectations. Adding to the caution, UnitedHealth expects FY 2026 revenue of $439 billion, well below the $456 billion analyst consensus.

data.title: Meta Stock Rises Even as Elon Musk Declares that 'WhatsApp Is Not Secure'
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) shares climbed slightly in pre-market trading on Tuesday, even as Elon Musk launched a viral attack on the company's privacy standards. The stock is currently benefiting from a pre-earnings run-up as investors anticipate a strong holiday revenue report on Wednesday. Wall Street remains focused on Meta's massive AI-driven ad growth, choosing to overlook the escalating war of words between Musk and Mark Zuckerberg.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Musk Declares 'WhatsApp Is Not Secure'

The controversy began when Musk shared a report detailing a new lawsuit filed in San Francisco. The legal filing alleges that Meta employees can bypass encryption using internal "task" requests to read private messages in real-time. Musk was direct in his response on X, stating:

"WhatsApp is not secure. Even Signal is questionable. Use X Chat."

This move is a direct hit to Meta's reputation, especially in markets like India and Brazil where WhatsApp is the primary tool for both personal and business life. Musk's skepticism toward Signal, an app he once famously recommended, suggests he is now positioning X as the final frontier for secure, un-monitored communication.

Meta and WhatsApp Execs Push Back

Leadership at Meta was quick to counter the narrative. Will Cathcart, the head of WhatsApp, directly replied to Musk's post to set the record straight. He argued that encryption keys are stored strictly on users' phones and that the company has no mathematical way to read messages. Cathcart labeled the lawsuit as a "no-merit, headline-seeking" attempt by a law firm with a history of defending controversial spyware.

Meta spokesperson Andy Stone echoed this sentiment, calling the claims "absurd." Through a decade of using the Signal Protocol, Meta has built its brand on the promise of privacy. The company maintains that the "backdoor" access described in the lawsuit simply doesn't exist, framing the legal challenge as a "work of fiction" designed to manipulate the stock price.

Why Meta Stock Is Rising Anyway

Despite the drama, the market is voting with its wallet. Meta stock is rising because of a "buy-the-rumor" sentiment ahead of Wednesday's earnings call. Analysts expect revenue to hit $58.4 billion, a 21% jump driven by high holiday ad demand. Investors are betting that the money machine of Instagram Reels and AI-targeted ads is far more important to the company's future than a social media spat.

Is Meta a Good Stock to Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, META stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 38 Buy, six Hold, and one Sell recommendations. The average Meta Platforms stock price target of $821.11 implies an upside potential of 22%.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: AT&T (T) Is About to Report Q4 Earnings. Options Traders Are Bracing for a 4.52% Move in the Telecom Stock
data.description:

While there are concerns about stiff competition and higher debt levels, several analysts are optimistic about AT&T due to the demand for bundled 5G wireless and fiber broadband plans and initiatives to bolster its network, including a $23 billion deal to acquire wireless spectrum licenses from EchoStar (SATS). data.title: How to Use a Stock Screener to Find Top-Performing Stocks: A Step-by-Step Guide
data.description:
Finding the right stocks for your investment portfolio can be a Herculean task. There are over 10,000 listed companies on the market from all sectors of industry, finance, retail and technology and from all geographies jostling for prominence and your attention.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Among all those red and green colors, up and down arrows and flashing screens, determining the winners from the losers, the next big things or the companies where the sun is setting is a little like finding a needle in a haystack.

Well, there is a tool available to help you in your search for that needle, and it doesn't involve magnifying glasses or a metal detector.

A stock screener can help you find the best stocks to buy in seconds. It is the ultimate filter narrowing that huge and noisy market down to the selections you need to meet your specific investment strategy or goals.

In this article we will explain how to use a stock screener and point you in the direction of the TipRanks Stock Screener. There you can find top stocks, analyst price targets, market consensus, dividend yields and PE/ratios all backed by Wall Street data to help you select stocks.

While others try and wade through the market like a busy, Saturday evening in Times Square, you will be strolling on a calm, uncrowded Californian beach.

What is a Stock Screener (and Why You Need One)

A stock screener is a stock market research tool which allows investors to filter and find stocks based on their own fundamental, technical and expert-driven criteria. The tool uses your criteria to screen the most appropriate stocks from its vast database of listed companies.

So, if you want a large cap stock or a mid-cap stock, if you want a stock from a specific sector or ones which are sitting at all-time highs or lows then it will find you the best matches with the press of a button.

Investors choose to use stock screener tools because it is a much more efficient way of finding the best stocks for their goals. A stock screener's filter can save an investor from hours and hours of manual research. It helps cut through the noise of the stock markets, ditching the dodgy feedback and highlighting the best tunes.

It also provides investors with the objective analysis that can be vital in the investing world. This means relying on data-driven filters and expertise rather than emotional bias. That means that the screener makes its choices solely on tangible data – revenues, market value, volatility etc – rather than a belief or a feeling that a certain company makes the best products on the market or has a strong and charismatic leadership team.

Getting Started – Setting Your Core Strategy

  • Identify Your Goals: A stock screener is only as good as the information that is provided to it. A little like asking your children to pop to the local shops for you without first drawing up a shopping list. Instead of the bread and milk you wanted, you get biscuits, ice cream and some animal inflatable. So, before you use a stock screener you need to identify exactly what you want it to screen for and why. Are you looking for long-term growth, dividend income or is it short-term day trading momentum you are after?
  • Key Basic Filters (Free Features) – Stock screeners offer a range of basic, free features to help investors get started. The main ones are listed below:
    • Market Cap – This will filter stocks on the basis of whether you are looking for stable, income producing large cap stocks or perhaps faster growing small caps.
    • Sector/Industry – This can help narrow down your selection to certain sectors or industries. It could be trendy, highly-valued AI or tech stocks or fast-growth/steady healthcare stocks. It could be industries which analysts have strong price targets for with hopes of further growth ahead or sectors with consistent dividend payers.
    • Price & Volume – If you're focus is on liquidity – being able to exit or enter positions quickly – then a stock screener can find the most appropriate stocks based on price and trading volume data.

Beyond the Basics: Finding "Best Stocks" with TipRanks Premium

Although there are a number of free stock screeners available, investors will have to subscribe if they want access to the most cutting-edge filtering technology. This will help ensure that they are doing absolutely everything to get an edge in often complex and confusing markets.

One example is the TipRanks Premium Stock Screener service. It has all the basic services available such as being able to search by market cap, sector, analyst consensus, P/E ratio and more.

But it also has some additional services for the Premium user, such as:

  • Analyst Price Targets – The TipRanks stock screener allows investors to search for stock upsides and downsides in %. What does this mean? An upside shows the difference between the current stock price and what Wall Street experts believe it will be in 12 months time. The bigger the upside the more attractive the stock and in contrast, the bigger the downside the more risk there will be. Although, based on other variables, that could still be the right buy for the right investor.
  • Analyst Consensus – On more basic screeners an investor can search for which stocks analysts believe are the best buys or the best sells. TipRanks premium allows the investor to dig deeper. They can filter for Strong Buy ratings, Moderate Buy, Hold, Moderate Sell or Strong Sell ratings. Again these are all ratings provided by the world's top performing analysts.
  • TR Smart Score – (The AI Advantage) – AI is increasingly becoming part of our everyday lives including our investment choices. The Tip Ranks Smart Score is a proprietary quantitative stock scoring system created by TipRanks. It gives stocks a score from one to ten, based on 8 key market factors. The lowest score is a 1 and the highest is 10. The scoring system works as follows: Stocks with a score of 8, 9, or 10 are considered Outperform. Stocks with a score of 4, 5, 6, or 7 are considered Neutral. Stocks with a score of 1, 2, or 3 are considered Underperform.

Advanced Screening Strategies (Actionable Templates)

Here are some strategic examples for investors using the TipRanks Premium screener.

  • Strategy A – An investor wants to filter for Wall Street Favorites. These are companies with a High Upside and a Strong Buy consensus. The filters to be used in this scenario would be – Analyst Consensus Strong Buy and Price Target Upside >20%. As seen below, the investor will have a top five list of Alphabet (GOOGL), Broadcom (AVGO), ASML (ASML), Chevron (CVX) and Applied Materials (AMAT).
data.author: David Craik
